item.title: Sum of Numbers Between Two Integers
item.description: Sum of Numbers Between Two Integers # Let \(a, b \in \mathbb{Z}\) , with \( b > a \) , be any two given integers. The sum of all integers between these two number, \(S\) , is given by \[S = \frac{(b-a+1)(a+b)}{2}.\] Proof # Note that the sum \( S \) can be written in two equivalent forms \[S = a + (a+1) + \dots + b,\] \[S = b + (b-1) + \dots + a.
